params.name: Open Rotor Engine
params.description:
On May 28, 2009 AD, NASA first tested an Open Rotor Engine.
Open Rotor Engine, also known as the propfan engine, is a type of turbine engine without a fan duct. Although it looks very similar to a turboprop engine, it has a very high propeller speed because it has no gearbox. It has a speed and performance similar to that of a turbofan engine, but in terms of economy, it is closer to a turboprop engine...
